mockResult.title: Sorting Algorithms & Complexity
mockResult.subject: Informatics Grade 11
mockResult.definition: Sorting is the process of arranging elements of a list in a certain order (ascending or descending).
mockResult.quickSort: QuickSort: O(n log n), most efficient.
mockResult.takeaway: QuickSort can drop to O(n^2) if pivot selection is poor. MergeSort consumes more memory due to recursion and requirement for temporary arrays.

c1.title: Rational Numbers
c1.content: Rational numbers are numbers that can be written as a/b where a, b are integers and b is not 0.

c2.title: Decimals
c2.content: How to convert between finite decimals and repeating decimals.

keyTakeaways.t1: Master the rules for addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of rational numbers.
keyTakeaways.t2: Understand the absolute value of a rational number.
